summary: Stellar Box of Rain opener, blazing Green Onions & tight jams
blurb: The set opens with a rain‑kissed Box of Rain that sets a magical mood, then rolls into a fiery second‑set sequence of Green Onions into China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider. Highlights include an intense The Other One jam and heartfelt Believe It Or Not.
review: June 30, 1988 at Silver Stadium in Rochester delivered one of the late‑‘80s’ most rewarding nights. After a rain‑stopped Box of Rain that opened the first set with crystalline clarity, the band kept the momentum tight through Cold Rain And Snow and New Minglewood Blues before diving into a second‑set that truly shines. A scorching Green Onions launch segues flawlessly into China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, while Brent’s Hammond wizardry drives the groove. Jerry’s guitar is both sweet and adventurous—he chops through He's Gone and belts an emotive Believe It Or Not—while Bill and Mickey lock in a blazing drum partnership that fuels the explosive The Other One/Wharf Rat climax. The show closes on a heartfelt Turn On Your Lovelight and a gentle Brokedown Palace encore, making this night a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six analyzed recordings carry a positive sentiment, repeatedly praising the high‑energy second set, standout jams (Green Onions, China Cat > Rider, The Other One), and strong individual performances. No negative consensus appears, so a strong positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard with superb instrument separation; captures the iconic Box of Rain opener and the blazing Green Onions > China Cat Sunflower > Rider sequence perfectly.

key_highlights: Rain‑kissed Box of Rain opener sets a magical mood
Green Onions launch into a fiery China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider jam
Intense The Other One improvisation leading into Wharf Rat
Brent's Hammond magic on Green Onions and Far From Me solo

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers crisp, blazing tone and strong vocal work; adventurous guitar on He's Gone and emotive lead on Believe It Or Not, often described as sweet and on fire.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation despite occasional mic/volume quirks; vocals on Box of Rain appreciated.
band_performance.Bob: Steady rhythm guitar with solid slide work after Minglewood and strong harmonies on Lovelight; overall reliable performance.
band_performance.Brent: On his A‑game, providing Hammond magic on Green Onions, a standout Far From Me solo, and supportive grooves throughout the jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides tight, supportive percussion that underpins the high‑energy second set, complementing Billy's driving beats.
band_performance.Billy: Blazing drumming especially during the Green Onions > China Cat Sunflower > Rider transition; matches Jerry’s surging power and fuels The Other One climax.
